title: dactylaena pohliana
intro: Dactylaena pohliana, also known as the poha tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. Traditionally used in folk medicine, Dactylaena pohliana has been found to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its saponins and flavonoids content. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including relief from fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and blood pressure. Dactylaena pohliana is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts can also be used to create teas, tinctures, and other herbal preparations.
